title: In praise of dark mode
speakers: Mehmet Aydın Baytaş (Senior Design Engineer, Attio)
key_points: Argues for 'Dark Mode Maximalism', the thesis that all user interfaces should be designed in dark mode, premised on the idea that good design emits as little light as possible.
Reframes dark mode not as a user-selectable toggle but as the default condition of good design itself.
Draws on the speaker's two decades of combined design and engineering practice, as well as 'arcane scholarship,' to support the case.
